pattern: Transpose the words column by column using each word's character at that column index, padding shorter words with spaces, then strip trailing spaces from each column string.
hints: Split the sentence into words and find the maximum word length — that is how many rows the vertical output has.
For each row index r, collect character r from each word (or a space if the word is shorter than r+1 characters).
Concatenate those characters into the row string, then remove trailing spaces with rstrip.

edge_inputs.description: three equal-length words — no padding needed
words of very different lengths create rows with interior spaces
input: TO BE OR NOT TO BE

edge_inputs.description: single word — result is its individual characters
last word is the longest — last rows have leading spaces that must be kept
one very long word followed by short words

stress.description: random sentence of up to 15 words each 1-12 chars long
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    num_words = max(1, size)
    words = []
    for _ in range(num_words):
        length = rng.randint(1, 12)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_uppercase) for _ in range(length)))
    return [' '.join(words)]
